stories.title: Beachum Watch — Day 13, Two Days Left
stories.angle: Jaysoni Beachum (.417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP / career 37 HR / 180 RBI / 202 H) is still uncommitted entering Day 13 of the 15-day transfer portal window. The Softball America No. 1 rated transfer has visited the Florida Gators among others. With the portal closing June 22, two days remain. She is the last elite name on the board. Every college softball program following the portal is watching the same person.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Jaysoni Beachum still uncommitted.

Day 13 of 15. Portal closes June 22 — two days left.

.417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP. One year eligible.

The No. 1 transfer is running out of time. ⏳

#CollegeSoftball #TransferPortal

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Two days. One question.

Where does Jaysoni Beachum land?

She visited Florida. She's the No. 1 transfer. The portal closes Sunday.

History says: the last one to decide picks exactly right. 🔥

#Beachum #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Two days. That's what's left.

The NCAA softball transfer portal window closes Sunday, June 22 — and the last elite name still uncommitted is Jaysoni Beachum.

She's the Softball America No. 1 rated transfer. She hit .417 with 16 home runs, 66 RBI, and a .549 OBP in 2026 at Florida State. She has 37 career home runs, 180 RBIs, and 202 hits. One year of eligibility left and a decision that will shape which program walks into 2027 with the biggest roster upgrade of anyone.

She visited Florida this week — her former team's biggest in-state rival. Whether it's the Gators or someone else, a decision has to come by Sunday.

Thirteen days into a 15-day window. One question left unanswered. The countdown is real. ⏳
facebook.image_caption: Day 13. Beachum still uncommitted. Two days left. Who's she picking? ⏳ #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Ella Dodge Commits to FSU — Tennessee Transfer Chooses Seminoles
stories.angle: Former Tennessee infielder Ella Dodge (.285 BA / 11 HR / 46 RBI in 2026 / ranked No. 10 overall in portal / No. 4 middle infielder) committed to Florida State on Friday. A redshirt junior with two seasons remaining and a Florida native from Bradenton, Dodge becomes FSU's answer to the Beachum and Torres departures. The Seminoles can't replace what left — but they can rebuild.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
COMMITTED: Ella Dodge is headed to Florida State. 🍢

The former Tennessee infielder (No. 10 overall in portal / No. 4 middle infielder) picks the Seminoles — two years of eligibility, Florida native, returning home.

FSU's rebuild starts now. 🔥

#FSUSoftball #TransferPortal

facebook.long_form:
Florida State is starting to answer back. 🍢

Former Tennessee infielder Ella Dodge announced Friday that she's committed to the Seminoles — bringing two years of eligibility, SEC-tested production, and a Florida-native connection that makes the fit feel right.

Dodge hit .285 with 11 home runs and 46 RBI in 2026 as a sophomore at Tennessee, making 64 starts and ranking as the No. 10 overall transfer in the portal and the No. 4 middle infielder. She grew up in Bradenton, Florida — not far from Tallahassee — and now she's heading home to help FSU rebuild.

After losing Isa Torres to Texas and watching Jaysoni Beachum still weigh her options, the Seminoles needed a signal that the program wasn't standing still. Ella Dodge is that signal.

She's not a replacement. She's a building block. 🧱
facebook.image_caption: Ella Dodge is headed to Tallahassee. FSU's rebuild has its first piece. 🍢 #FSUSoftball

stories.title: FSU Rebuilds After Losing Two Stars — Dodge Is the First Piece
stories.angle: With Isa Torres at Texas and Jaysoni Beachum still searching, Florida State faces the task of rebuilding without its two most decorated 2026 players. Ella Dodge's commitment is the first step — a top-10 portal player who plays a position FSU desperately needs. The Seminoles also pick up Ella Dodge's two-year window of eligibility. What FSU's 2027 roster looks like, and how they're approaching the rebuild after a portal that hit them hard.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Florida State lost Isa Torres (.530 BA / D1 POY) and Jaysoni Beachum (No. 1 transfer) this portal.

Now they've added Ella Dodge — No. 10 overall, SEC-tested, two years left.

It's not a replacement. It's a foundation. 🧱

#FSUSoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
When Isa Torres posted "I'm coming home" in burnt orange, FSU fans felt it.

When Jaysoni Beachum visited Florida — FSU's biggest in-state rival — and is still deciding, they felt that too.

This has been a portal window that hit Florida State hard. Torres was their D1 Player of the Year. Beachum was Softball America's No. 1 rated transfer. Together, they represented the peak of what the Seminoles had built over three seasons. And now they're both gone from Tallahassee.

But Florida State isn't sitting still. Ella Dodge's commitment on Friday was Step One of a rebuild that needed to start immediately. She's a top-10 portal player with two years remaining and the kind of SEC background that shows she can compete at the highest level.

The Seminoles have 48 hours of portal window left. Whether they add another piece or not, the direction is clear: FSU is playing the long game, and it starts with Dodge.

The next chapter in Tallahassee isn't a repeat. It's a rebuild. And rebuilds can become dynasties. 💪
facebook.image_caption: After Torres and Beachum, FSU adds Dodge. The rebuild is on. 💪 #FSUSoftball #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Who Won the 2026 Transfer Portal? — Breaking Down the Big Programs
stories.angle: With two days to go, the 2026 transfer portal verdict is nearly in. Texas added Isa Torres, Swan, and Lincoln — the ultimate class. Texas Tech assembled three All-Americans (Polar, Heavener, Pickering). Arkansas gained Yarnell and Wetteland. Tennessee added Shelton and brought Mardjetko back. With 79+ players committed out of 120 in the portal, every major program got something — some got everything.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Who won the 2026 transfer portal?

Texas: Torres + Swan + Lincoln 🤘
Texas Tech: Polar + Heavener + Pickering 🔴

Vote below — and the portal isn't even closed yet. 😤

#CollegeSoftball #TransferPortal

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Don't sleep on Arkansas.

Wetteland (1.95 ERA, Mountain West FOTY) + Yarnell (.344 BA / 15 HR / 46 RBI) quietly built one of the best portal hauls of June 2026. 👀

#ArkansasSoftball #TransferPortal

facebook.long_form:
The 2026 transfer portal window is almost over — and before it slams shut on Sunday, let's talk about who really won it.

Texas gets the narrative crown. Isa Torres (.530 BA / D1 Player of the Year) plus catcher Samantha Swan and pitcher Samantha Lincoln? That's not a portal class, that's a statement. The two-time defending champions just added the best player in college softball. Their floor in 2027 is higher than anyone else's ceiling.

Texas Tech gets the Softball America No. 1 class designation. Moriah Polar (.555 BA / 101 hits), Jayden Heavener (No. 1 pitcher, 2.81 ERA / 128 Ks), and Kasidi Pickering (50 career HRs from Oklahoma). A 61-8 WCWS runner-up added three All-Americans. The Red Raiders could be better in 2027 than they were in 2026.

Arkansas is the quiet winner. Madison Wetteland (Mountain West FOTY, 1.95 ERA) and Kendall Yarnell (.344 BA / 15 HR) give the Razorbacks a boost on both sides. Sleep on them at your own risk.

Tennessee didn't chase stars — they added Karley Shelton and brought back Sage Mardjetko for a senior season. That's a reload, not a rebuild.

Then there's FSU — whose portal went in reverse. They gave away two stars and are starting over with Ella Dodge. Still 48 hours to change the picture.

Two days left. One big name (Beachum) still undecided. The portal finale is Sunday. 📋
facebook.image_caption: Texas? Texas Tech? Arkansas? Who really won the 2026 transfer portal? The window closes Sunday. 📋 #CollegeSoftball

research.facts:
Who Won the 2026 Transfer Portal?
**Classification:** NEW STORY (portal window analysis — nearly closed)
**Sources:**
- On3 2026-27 Softball Transfer Portal tracker
- Mountain West/Pac-12 tracker (Yahoo Sports, June 19)
- Individual commitment articles from prior pipeline runs
- SI/OnSoftball portal rankings and trackers
**Key Facts:**
- Portal open June 8–22, 120+ players entered, 79+ committed (66%+)
- Texas class: Torres (D1 POY), Swan (ASU catcher), Lincoln (Texas Tech pitcher), Altmeyer — consensus winner
- Texas Tech class: Polar (.555 BA / 101 hits), Heavener (No. 1 pitcher, 2.81 ERA/128K), Pickering (50 career HRs) — Softball America No. 1 class
- Arkansas: Yarnell (UCF, .344 BA/15HR/46RBI), Wetteland (Mountain West FOTY, 1.95 ERA)
- Tennessee: Shelton (SC transfer .345 BA / 40 RBI) + Mardjetko returns senior
- FSU: Dodge (No. 10 overall) + hoping for another move before June 22
- Oklahoma: picking up depth (Adi Hansen, College of Southern Idaho OF)
- Still out: Beachum (No. 1 overall)
**Freshness:** HIGH for Texas, TTU, Arkansas, Tennessee (confirmed in prior pipelines and from sources above). Dodge fresh (June 19). Oklahoma/Adi Hansen from search. Analysis is current as of Day 13.

stories.title: Portal Tracker — Day 13 of 15, Final 48 Hours
stories.angle: Day 13 of 15. Two days remain before the June 22 close. Committed class summary: Torres (TX), Swan (TX), Lincoln (TX), Altmeyer (TX), Polar (TTU), Heavener (TTU), Pickering (TTU), Hartley (OSU), Wetteland (Ark), Yarnell (Ark), Azua (A&M), Plumlee (Ala), Shelton (Tenn), Dodge (FSU). One name still out: Beachum. The final 48 hours belong to one question.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Portal Tracker — Day 13 of 15 ⏳

✅ Texas: Torres, Swan, Lincoln
✅ Texas Tech: Polar, Heavener, Pickering
✅ FSU: Dodge | Ark: Wetteland, Yarnell
✅ Hartley (OSU) | Shelton (Tenn)

Still out: Beachum (No. 1 transfer)

June 22 = portal closes. Two days.

#CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Day 13 of 15. Two days remain before the NCAA softball transfer portal closes Sunday, June 22.

Here's where we stand as of Saturday morning:

Texas locked up: Isa Torres (D1 POY), Samantha Swan, Samantha Lincoln, plus Altmeyer. Best individual name in portal class.

Texas Tech secured: Moriah Polar, Jayden Heavener, Kasidi Pickering. Softball America's No. 1 class nationally.

Others confirmed: Hartley (OSU) / Wetteland + Yarnell (Arkansas) / Azua (Texas A&M) / Plumlee (Alabama) / Shelton (Tennessee) / Dodge (Florida State — Friday commit).

And then there's Jaysoni Beachum.

She's still out there. Softball America No. 1. .417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP. One year left. Visited Florida. No announcement yet.

Forty-eight hours remain. The final chapter of the 2026 transfer portal is still being written. 📋
facebook.image_caption: Day 13 of 15. The portal closes Sunday. Here's who's in and who's still out. 📋 #CollegeSoftball #TransferPortal
